source_intake_record.raw_question_text: The S-parameters of a two-port network is given as \( [S] = \begin{bmatrix} S_{11} & S_{12} \\ S_{21} & S_{22} \end{bmatrix} \) with reference to \( Z_0 \). Two lossless transmission line sections of electrical lengths \( \theta_1 = \beta l_1 \) and \( \theta_2 = \beta l_2 \) are added to the input and output ports for measurement purposes, respectively. The S-parameters \( [S'] \) of the resultant two-port network is

multi_step: the task asks for the transformed S-parameter matrix after adding two transmission-line sections, which requires relating port extensions to phase shifts
different entries of the 2x2 matrix receive different phase factors: diagonal terms depend on doubled single-port electrical lengths, while off-diagonal terms depend on the sum of both lengths
the answer is not a single direct lookup from the prompt; it involves structured symbolic transformation across multiple matrix elements
verifiability: answer_text is an explicit symbolic 2x2 matrix, so each entry can be checked element-wise
